title: Goniopteris gonophora
taxonomy: Goniopteris gonophora is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae and the order Polypodiales. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida and the division Pteridophyta. This classification is based on its characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other ferns. The genus Goniopteris is a group of ferns that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Goniopteris gonophora is a specific species within this genus, and its classification helps us understand its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.

Its leaves and stems are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In some cultures, the plant is used as a treatment for digestive issues and to reduce swelling. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
